task: Verify additional MCP servers (notebooklm, fetch, filesystem)

key_learnings: Filesystem MCP is fully active and successfully sandboxed, restricting allowed operations to designated work paths ('D:\MidpointX', 'D:\Reports').

Fetch MCP uses pure-Python mode for article layout extraction when Node/NPM dependencies are missing on the local interpreter. Fetching empty HTML/API bodies (like httpbin status codes) triggers index range errors, whereas standard HTML pages (like example.com) parse flawlessly.
NotebookLM MCP is fully logged in and verified. It successfully communicates with the Google NotebookLM backend, retrieving personal notebooks, counts, and ownership metadata.
outcomes.patterns_observed: Health checks for fetch MCP tools should target standard HTML pages with readable text structure to avoid pure-Python parser edge-case exceptions.
